issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the default focus outline (outline: none) without providing any visible alternative such as a box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline. Keyboard‑only users lose the focus indicator, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline with outline: none and provides only an outline‑offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for button elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without any replacement, making the mobile navigation toggle invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.explanation: The submit button in the site‑search form removes its outline without providing an alternative, making it invisible to keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons suppress the focus outline with outline: none and outline‑offset only, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline without any replacement, breaking keyboard accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG minimum touch target size of 24 px, making it difficult for finger users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class defines min-width and min-height of 20px, both below the required 24 px minimum, reducing accessibility for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links without providing any other visual cue (e.g., color change, border, or font‑weight), making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for many users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector applies a transform transition that moves the image on hover, but there is no reduced‑motion override disabling this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class includes a transform on `:active` (translateY(1px)) and a transition that animates it, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this for users preferring reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` applies a transform (translateY(-1px)) without a reduced‑motion override, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller shows and hides panels by adding/removing CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but it never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance add trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true') and in _closeInstance add trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false') (or toggle via setAttr).  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria‑pressed (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (element [data‑billing‑toggle]) changes visual state but never updates aria‑pressed to reflect the on/off state, leaving assistive technology unaware of the toggle status.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling the visual state, call setAttr(toggleEl,'aria-pressed', isYear ? 'true' : 'false') and set the same on toggleTrack if used as a switch.  

---

issuesFound.title: ARIA‑expanded not initialised for dropdown triggers (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger buttons receive aria‑haspopup but lack an initial aria‑expanded value, violating the requirement that the attribute be present before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false')` during registration.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic filter count not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters the product count badge is updated, but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`)`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change not announced (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/annual billing updates UI but does not provide a live‑region announcement, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Annual billing selected')` or similar.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item updates ARIA expanded but does not announce the state change via a live region, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting aria‑expanded, call `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – ${item.open?'expanded':'collapsed'}`)`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top activation not announced (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the “back‑to‑top” button scrolls the page but does not announce the action, so users of assistive technology may not know the page moved.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page')` after the scroll.  

---

issuesFound.title: Live‑region update missing after product‑filter view‑mode toggle (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Changing between grid and list view updates classes but does not inform screen‑reader users of the new view mode.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce(viewMode==='grid' ? 'Grid view activated' : 'List view activated')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and auto dimensions instead of absolute positioning and clipping, making the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility class.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition and hover transform effect (perspective rotation), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, providing no override for users who prefer reduced motion. This can cause vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transform: translateY(1px) active state animation and transition on transform property, but no reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular sensitivities.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on product cards
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards have a transform: translateY(-3px) hover animation with transition, but no reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on pricing cards
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards have transform hover animations (translateY and scale) without a reduced-motion override to disable these spatial movements for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% white) text on a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table cells for "Not included" and "Not available" use var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) text on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute. This leaves the field incorrectly marked as invalid after the user has corrected the input.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register missing aria-expanded initialization

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters to the product grid, the visible content updates and the product count changes, but no announcement is made to screen reader users regarding the filter results or the number of products now displayed.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismiss without user control
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a configurable duration using setTimeout without providing users a mechanism to pause, stop, or extend the display time. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) for users who require more time to read notifications.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1367: Replace the setTimeout block with user-controllable dismissal: Add `on(toast, 'mouseenter', () => clearTimeout(timerId)); on(toast, 'mouseleave', () => timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur));` and ensure a manual dismiss button is always present (already present in code).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving into the drawer. Keyboard users must tab through the entire page content to reach the navigation links, and screen reader users may not realize the menu content is available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1625: Insert `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` immediately before the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel auto-play without pause on focus
issuesFound.explanation: While the carousel pauses on hover, the auto-play timer continues running when focus moves within the carousel via keyboard navigation. Users navigating with keyboards do not have the same pause behavior as mouse users, creating inconsistent accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2484: Add `pause();` inside the focusin handler immediately after the line `_rootEl.addEventListener('focusin',     pause);` and ensure play only resumes on focusout if the focus has left the entire carousel (already partially implemented but ensure strict pause on any focusin).
